page: Google Cloud Platform c3-highmem-44 is a memory-optimized server instance powered by an Intel Xeon 8481C processor. It features 44 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 22 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, running at a base clock speed of 2.7 GHz. The system is equipped with 352 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8 GB per core. The CPU architecture is x86_64, incorporating a 105 MB L3 cache, 2 MB L2 cache, and 48 KB L1d cache. This instance does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, and it offers zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark results place the instance in the middle 50% for standard compute and memory tasks, with a Geekbench multi-core score of 15596.0 and a PassMark CPU score of 47633.9. However, it demonstrates strong performance in specific workloads, ranking in the top 25% for static web serving throughput at 41.56 GB/sec, image processing, and CPU encryption. The hardware features advanced instruction sets, including Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) and AVX-512, which accelerate low-latency operations. For small to medium language models, prompt processing is highly efficient, reaching 2533.54 tokens per second on a 135M model. Conversely, text generation for large 70B models is a notable bottleneck, yielding a poor performance of 2.41 tokens per second due to the lack of dedicated GPU acceleration.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the c3-highmem-44 offers a dense memory configuration relative to its CPU resources, making it a viable alternative to GPU-accelerated instances for memory-bound workloads that do not require massive parallel processing. It is well-suited for high-throughput web serving, in-memory databases, enterprise application hosting, and moderate machine learning inference tasks utilizing CPU-optimized instruction sets like AMX.
description: Google Cloud Platform c3-highmem-44 is a memory-optimized server configured with 44 dedicated vCPUs and 352 GB of RAM, yielding an 8 GB per vCPU ratio. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8481C processor running at 2.7 GHz, this x86_64 instance features advanced instruction sets including Intel AMX and AVX-512. While it lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, it delivers strong performance in static web serving, image processing, and cryptographic operations. Benchmark data indicates average multi-core capabilities but highlights strong prompt processing speeds for small to medium language models. However, text generation for large 70B models is a performance bottleneck. This instance is highly suitable for memory-intensive workloads, in-memory databases, and web serving applications that benefit from high memory density without requiring dedicated GPU hardware.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c3-highmem-44 is a memory-optimized server featuring 44 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 352 GB of RAM, and advanced AMX instruction sets, ideal for in-memory databases and web serving.
